sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Vaginal Candidiasis – 10
sources.population: Patients with complaints compatible with vaginal candidiasis.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for candidiasis-compatible vaginal-discharge documentation in Dubai telehealth; partial for bacterial vaginosis, STI, cervical, pelvic, postmenopausal, pregnancy, and other discharge causes.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability; 6. Risk Factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the Dubai telehealth population and diabetes, antibiotic, estrogen, pregnancy, immunosuppression, medication, and device risk contexts.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Clinical History
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies discharge character, itching, soreness, dyspareunia, dysuria, onset, duration, recurrence, menstrual and abnormal bleeding, pregnancy, medical, medication, and sexual history.

exact_sections.heading: 8. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists lower abdominal pain with fever, foul-smelling or blood-stained discharge, postcoital bleeding, intermenstrual bleeding, and recent-partner context.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Investigations; 13. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines when microscopy or culture is not routine, when targeted culture is appropriate, and when pregnancy, systemic illness, atypical symptoms, recurrence, bleeding, or lower abdominal pain require face-to-face or specialist review.
